The prophet must announce that ruin is coming upon Judah and Jerusalem. Both leaders and the people must pay attention to it. That place which holiness made the joy of the whole earth, sin made the reproach and shame of the whole earth. There is no escaping God's justice, but by fleeing to his mercy.

The potter’s vessel, after it is hardened, cannot be pieced again when it is broken. And as the bottle was broken, so shall Judah and Jerusalem be broken by the Chaldeans. No human hand can repair it; but if they return to the Lord he will heal. As they filled Tophet with the slain sacrificed to their idols, so will God fill the whole city with the slain that shall fall as sacrifices to his justice. Whatever people may think, God will appear as terrible against sin and sinners as the Scriptures state; nor will the unbelief of people make his promise or his threatenings ineffective. The obstinacy of sinners in sinful ways is their own fault; if they are deaf to the word of God, it is because they have stopped their ears. We need to pray that God, by his grace, would deliver us from hardness of heart and contempt of his word and commandments.
